text:
AISHA AND THE SANDS OF 
DESTINY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Songs & Music Production Package — Complete (Acts 1–11)
Total: 9 Songs
Animated Film — Music Production Document

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
SONG 1 — "A THOUSAND WONDERS"
Act 2 | Pages 13–15
Singer(s): Aisha (lead), Dora (verse 2 + alternating verse 3), both together (chorus)
SECTION 1 — LYRICS
VERSE 1 — Aisha 
AISHA: 
Every color, every sound, 
Like a story spun around— 
Worlds I’ve only dreamed about, 
Now bloom before my eyes. 
(Instrumental bridge) 
Spices rise into the air, 
Silks like sunsets everywhere, 
I could wander, I could dare, 
Beneath a thousand skies… 
VERSE 2 (DORA, comical character happy and pleased):
I smell almonds, roasted sweet,
Pistachios lost beneath my feet—
This whole place is one big treat,
A feast for royal paws!!!
Velvet cushions, all hand-made,
Silks that never crease or fade—
Why chase trouble? I’d prefer,
A life of snacks and perfect fur.! 
PRE-CHORUS (AISHA):
I never knew what lay outside,
Behind the walls where I was kept inside. 
And this is freedom’s gentle taste, 
I wonder what awaits beyond our gate… 
CHORUS (BOTH):
A thousand wonders, a thousand lights,
A thousand voices on a thousand nights.
Beyond the palace, past the walls —
A thousand wonders, a thousand calls!
VERSE 3 (ALTERNATING):
(AISHA): Lantern lights like twinkling stars—
(DORA in comical awe and joy): And grilled cheese wrapped in golden jars—
(AISHA):Every path leads someplace far—
(DORA teasingly comical): Preferably with snacks.
(AISHA): Maybe I was meant to roam—
Page  of 218

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
(DORA teasing looking in comical disapproval): Or maybe you should turn back home—
(AISHA): But something in my heart has grown—
(DORA softening): And I can’t hold you back…
CHORUS (BOTH):
A thousand wonders, a thousand lights,
A thousand voices on a thousand nights.
Beyond the palace, past the walls —
A thousand wonders, a thousand calls!
SECTION 2 — SCENE CONTEXT & ACTION SEQUENCE
This is Aisha's first time ever stepping into the city's Grand Market of Quazar Al'Zaman, the 
capital's legendary bazaar, filled with color, noise, spices, and life. Dora, her magical 
companion, is overwhelmed by the sensory feast. Aisha has spent her entire life behind palace 
walls, protected and sheltered by her father the Sultan.
The song begins with Aisha stepping through an ornate archway into the sunlit market. As she 
sings Verse 1, she moves through clusters of vendors and stalls — lifting lanterns, touching silk 
scarves, spinning with wonder, her eyes wide. Golden sunlight streams through woven canopy 
fabrics overhead. Each verse triggers a burst of magical action: Dora belly-flops onto a pile of 
velvet cushions; a merchant tosses her a plum and she eats it in one bite; Aisha spins and a silk 
scarf unfurls around her like a dancer's ribbon. The chorus explodes: both friends grab hands 
and dance through the market crowd, stalls erupting in confetti-like petals and spinning brass 
mobiles. Citizens laugh and clap. Lanterns float upward as Aisha's joy rises. The final chorus 
builds to a giant crescendo — Aisha and Dora stand on a market fountain, the sun glowing 
warm behind them, arms spread wide as the market bursts into joyful chaos around them.
SECTION 3 — SUNO AI MUSIC PROMPT
3D animated movie musical, upbeat joyful Disney-style market song, two female voices — lead 
voice is a young princess, tender mezzo-soprano, warm and emotional, not too high, American 
style; second voice is playful and comedic, slightly lower. Mood: pure wonder, first taste of 
freedom, childlike joy, comedic delight. Instrumentation: hand drums, oud, kora, mbira, layered 
strings, brass fanfares, African desert percussion with Middle Eastern melodic motifs — not purely 
Arabic, creative fusion mix. Style reference: Aladdin "Friend Like Me" energy meets Lion King 
African rhythms. Starts gentle and swells into a big joyful finale chorus. Bright, colorful, celebratory. 
Wordless backing choir joins the chorus.
Page  of 318

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
SONG 2 — "JUST ONE WISH"
Act 3 | Pages 25–26
Singer(s): Djinn  character (solo);
SECTION 1 — LYRICS
(VESRE 1)
 
From moonlit chains and ancient plains,  
your call has set me free
One spark of light, one ring held tight,  
and you awakened me 
(Instrumental Bridge) 
(VERSE 2) 
I've walked with storms, shaped shifting forms,  
I've danced on desert air
But one wish now, and not one more, 
So choose it with great care! 
(Instrumental bridge) 
(VERSE 3) 
No palace walls, no silken halls  
can cage a dream that burns
For one wish bold can break the mold  
of fate that twists and turns! 
(VERSE 4) 
So think not small or shy or slight,  
think wild as desert breeze
For one wish lifts the veils of night  
And bends the world with ease!… 
SECTION 2 — SCENE CONTEXT & ACTION SEQUENCE
It is deep night. Aisha is alone in her chamber. She slips on her magical desert-sand shoes for 
the first time — and they glow. The moonstone ring on her finger pulses with warm golden light. 
Suddenly the room erupts: a swirling column of golden sand shoots upward from the ring, and 
the Djinn materializes — tall, luminous, theatrical, floating with crossed arms and a mischievous 
grin.
The Djinn's song is a big comedic-yet-magical entrance number. As he sings, golden dunes rise 
from the bedroom floor; he becomes a silhouette of whirling sand, moving like smoke. He glides 
past Aisha upside-down, tapping her nose mid-lyric. He shapes the air into a glowing world map 
that unfurls like parchment across the ceiling. At "think wild as desert breeze" he gestures 
dramatically, and a curtain of stars explodes through the window. Dora, who has stumbled 
awake, builds a pillow fort and hides behind it, eyeing the Djinn with furious suspicion. Niura the 
snake watches from the windowsill, intrigued. The Djinn ends the song with a sweeping bow as 
Page  of 418

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
his illumination collapses back into a swirl of golden dust — leaving the room looking completely 
normal again. Only the glow of the ring remains.
SECTION 3 — SUNO AI MUSIC PROMPT
3D animated movie musical, big comedic villain-style entrance song for a magical Djinn character 
— cartoonish, theatrical, charismatic, playful. Solo male voice: deep-rich, theatrical baritone, 
operatic flair with comedic timing, like a stage magician who loves the spotlight. Mood: 
mischievous wonder, ancient power with a wink, magical showmanship. Instrumentation: oud 
fanfare opener, layered hand drums, bass kora, shimmering thumb piano, orchestral strings 
building to brass, cascading harps when magic appears. Creative fusion of African desert tradition 
and Middle Eastern flourish — no pure Arabic cliché. Style reference: Lion King villain theatrical 
energy meets Aladdin genie showmanship. Starts mysterious and builds to a big theatrical finish.
Page  of 518

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
SONG 3 — "BEASTS OF THUNDER"
Act 7 | Pages 83–86
Singer(s): The Prince (solo show song / villainous seduction, epic, powerful)
SECTION 1 — LYRICS
VERSE 1 (Prince commanding Thunder Panthers):
Beasts of the storm, born of thunder's command,
Bend to the whisper that steadies the land… 
(Powerful instrumental bridge) 
VERSE 2 (Prince commanding Highland Lynxes):
Fangs of the mountains, silent as night,
Stand at my side as keepers of might. 
(Powerful instrumental bridge) 
VERSE 3 (Prince commanding creatures Maz-Khars):
Run with the wind, in the air ascend,
Strength upon strength when destinies blend—
From earth into sky, let power take flight,
Together we blaze through the heart of the night!!!! 
(Long Instrumental epic bridge) 
FINAL REFRAIN (sung softly into the wind while flying): 
Two paths now crossing, two lights now aligned,
Carried by winds where our destinies bind…
SECTION 2 — SCENE CONTEXT & ACTION SEQUENCE
Inside a colossal hippodrome-like arena carved from golden stone at the edge of the desert — a 
fake city created by the Shadow King to trap Aisha. The Prince stages a spectacular show of 
power for Aisha, who sits in a crystal palanquin, hypnotized by a perfume enchantment, radiant 
but distant. Dora watches in suspicious horror from below.
The show is structured in three acts, each verse triggering a new creature. First: Thunder 
Panthers — five-times-panther-sized beasts with electric-blue streaks, sprinting the arena so 
fast they blur. The Prince sings the first verse and they halt, kneeling. The crowd explodes. 
Second: Highland Lynxes — enormous winter-white beasts with gold-tipped fur, marching 
beside royal warriors in perfect step. Third: Maz-Khars — sleek winged creatures with zebra-
stripe glowing fur, running like fire, then unfurling molten-glass wings mid-track and soaring over 
the arena in shimmering trails. The crowd reaches euphoria. Then two giant Maz-Khars emerge.  
The Prince invites Aisha to ride. She mounts — Dora screaming below. They ascend above the 
arena, the Prince singing his soft final refrain into the desert wind as they circle high above, 
dunes shimmering like liquid gold below. They land side by side to thunderous cheers. The 
magic is at its peak. The ring on Aisha's finger suddenly warms — the Djinn breaks through the 
false sky.
SECTION 3 — SUNO AI MUSIC PROMPT
Page  of 618

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
3D animated movie musical, powerful villain-style seduction showsong for a charming but 
dangerous Prince character. Solo male voice: rich confident tenor, smooth and commanding, like a 
showman king. Mood: spectacle, power, grandeur, seductive danger — feels like triumph but hides 
manipulation. Instrumentation: thunderous war drums, heroic brass fanfares, epic orchestral 
strings swelling on each beast reveal, African percussion base, Middle Eastern oud underscoring 
the danger beneath the beauty. Creative desert fusion — not purely Arabic, cinematic and epic. 
Style reference: Lion King "Be Prepared" theatricality meets Aladdin "Prince Ali" parade energy. 
Builds in three waves, each bigger, ending with soft tender refrain on winds.
Page  of 718

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
SONG 4 — "AISHA'S LULLABY"
Act 5 | Pages 52–53
Singer(s): Aisha (solo)
SECTION 1 — LYRICS
(Intro : wordless desert  motives ethereal voice soft intro)
VERSE 1:
Where the wind is soft and gentle
Where the moon leans close to hear
There my voice will always find you
There I keep you safe and near 
VERSE 2:
Every soul is tied by silver
Threads the moonlight cannot hide
Every mother leaves a river
Flowing quiet by your side 
VERSE 3:
If the silence ever frightens
If the shadows press too tight
Let your heart remember singing
I will answer with my light
(Soft humming melodically outro, like a magic beret themes inspired lullaby)
SECTION 2 — SCENE CONTEXT & ACTION SEQUENCE
The Silent City of Mayyala — a desert city whose citizens have had their voices stolen by the 
Shadow King. No one can speak or sing. The streets are haunted with mute grief. Aisha arrives 
and encounters a mother trying to comfort her crying infant — the mother opens her mouth but 
no sound comes out. She is weeping silently, desperate.
Aisha kneels beside them, and gently begins to sing the lullaby her own mother Zahra sang to 
her as a child. The song begins barely above a whisper. As she sings, the child slowly quiets. 
Nearby windows crack open — silhouetted faces appear, listening. With each verse, faint 
golden threads seem to spin from Aisha's voice, curling softly through the air. When she 
reaches Verse 3, one woman in the crowd opens her mouth — and her voice returns, a single 
trembling note. Tears stream down her face. She places her hand over her heart, overwhelmed. 
Aisha holds the child close as the last note fades. The crowd is still and reverent. Silence — but 
no longer the emptiness of stolen voices. This is a silence full of something returning.
SECTION 3 — SUNO AI MUSIC PROMPT
Page  of 818

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
3D animated movie musical, intimate solo lullaby for young princess character.Inpired by magic 
ethereal desert themes, ethnic celestial motives, tear-drawing motivational motives, mother 
memories . Tender mezzo-soprano female voice, warm and emotional, slow and gentle, American 
singer style — not too high, carries only a few notes with deep feeling. Mood: deep love, loss, 
gentle hope, quiet magic — like a prayer whispered in the dark. Instrumentation: single oud opens, 
joined by very soft kora plucks, cello undertone, warm bass notes, sparse African mbira in the 
background. Simple, spare, haunting. No loud percussion. Feels like moonlight. Style reference: 
Lion King emotional ballad — "Can You Feel the Love Tonight" tenderness but with ethnic Derek 
themes styles. Only the chorus carries slight harmonic lift. Fades like a breath.
Page  of 918

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
SONG 5 — "WAKE AGAIN"
Act 5 | Pages 54–55
Singer(s): Aisha (starts solo), Citizens of Mayyala join (layered epic chorus)
SECTION 1 — LYRICS
VERSE (AISHA):
Water sleeping under stone
Hear a voice you once had known
Rise and wash the fear we've grown
Find your path and flow back home… 
(Instrumental bridge) 
CHORUS (AISHA, then CITIZENS joining):
Wake again, Mayyala city
Lift your rivers, find your light
Join your hearts, your strength, your pity
Chase the shadows from the night 
Let the fountains sing together
Let the dust be swept away
May your voice return forever
May your spirit rise today!
SECTION 2 — SCENE CONTEXT & ACTION SEQUENCE
The Peacock Fountain Plaza at the heart of Mayyala, night. The city's great fountain — a tiered 
marble structure carved with peacock motifs — has been dry and silent since the Shadow King 
stole the citizens' voices and broke the city's spirit. Aisha stands alone at its base, looking up at 
the silent stone birds, moonlight catching the dry carved channels.
She begins to improvise — pressing her palm to the stone base and singing, not quite sure what 
she is doing, following some instinct deeper than knowledge. As her voice rises, the water inside 
the dry channels begins to stir. Tiny trickles appear. A window opens. An old man peers out. 
Another window. A young woman steps to the edge of her balcony. As the chorus lifts, the 
fountain erupts — jets of water shooting upward, catching the moonlight like crystal. Peacock 
shapes form in the water. Citizens pour into the plaza, opening their mouths — and their voices 
return, joining the chorus in waves. The city fills with sound for the first time in years. People 
weep and laugh. Children run through the fountains. Aisha spins in the water, laughing and 
crying at once. The song ends with the entire city singing together in one glowing, echoing mass 
of sound.
SECTION 3 — SUNO AI MUSIC PROMPT
Page  of 1018

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
3D animated movie musical, awakening anthem starting intimate then growing into massive 
layered chorus. Lead voice: young princess, tender mezzo-soprano, emotional and warm, 
American singer style. Starts solo, barely audible. As song progresses: layered citizen voices join 
— all ages, men and women, creating a rich symphonic vocal tapestry. Instrumentation: gentle oud 
intro, soft kora; then African percussion builds from single drum to full ensemble, strings rise, brass 
joins the finale. Choir swells to stadium scale. Style reference: Lion King "Circle of Life" spiritual-
communal choral power meets Aladdin's anthemic finale energy. African and Middle Eastern fusion 
— not purely Arabic, epic and universal. Ends in triumphant unison.
Page  of 1118

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
SONG 6 — "BEFORE THE SHADOW"
Act 8 | Pages 96–97
Singer(s): The Prince (starts solo), Aisha (joins mid-song), both sing final chorus together
SECTION 1 — LYRICS
VERSE 1 (PRINCE):
Before the shadow stole our dawn,
Before my people's hope was gone,
This land was light, this land was peace,
A kingdom warm as golden fleece. 
VERSE 2 (PRINCE):
But when he rose in poisoned night,
He turned our days to endless fright…
And so he bound my soul by chain,
To serve his darkness, bear his pain. 
VERSE 3 (PRINCE):
He made me Jinn, he stole my throne,
He left my people turned to stone.
I roamed in silence, torn apart…
Until your courage healed my heart. 
VERSE 4 (AISHA — joining):
I walked the world beyond my walls,
I felt its wounds, I heard its calls.
You showed me stars I'd never seen —
And now you stand where hopes begin. 
CHORUS (BOTH — floating dance):
So take my hand, though paths are dark,
We'll light the sand with just one spark.
Two souls choose to mend as one,
To heal the lands with hope and love.
SECTION 2 — SCENE CONTEXT & ACTION SEQUENCE
The ruins of Ramlat al-Jawhar — a once-majestic desert city, now broken stone and dry canals 
under the stars. Moonlight washes over cracked Nabatean carvings and a shimmering azure 
lake surrounded by a ruined plaza. The Djinn uses his second wish to reveal his true human 
form: a young Prince in deep maroon and burgundy royal kaftan, eyes dark and full of old grief.
As the Prince sings, each verse triggers a visual magic: at Verse 1, he brushes a ruined arch 
and it rebuilds into a pristine gateway — vines blooming with bioluminescent flowers. At Verse 2, 
a shadow-play rises from the ground, replaying the invasion, the fall, his enslavement. At Verse 
3, he turns to Aisha, voice softening. She steps forward, eyes shining, and begins Verse 4. She 
touches a ruined fountain — and light-water erupts, flowing like liquid diamonds across the 
plaza. He takes her hand. They begin a slow floating dance above the glowing water. Flowers 
bloom as they pass. Cacti open into radiant blossoms. The broken city reclaims its former glory 
around them. They end close — breath mingling, hearts open. Then CRASH — the magic 
collapses. Water fades to dust. He transforms back into the Djinn. The revelation is over. But 
both know something irreversible has passed between them.
Page  of 1218

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
SECTION 3 — SUNO AI MUSIC PROMPT
3D animated movie musical, emotional ballad duet — starts as heartbreaking solo lament then 
becomes a tender love duet. First voice: young male tenor, warm and aching, emotionally raw, 
carrying centuries of grief. Second voice: young princess mezzo-soprano, tender and brave, not 
too high — American style, slow and gentle. Together: a two-voice ballad with gentle harmonic 
interweaving, not operatic, intimate and real. Mood: grief and redemption, recognition, the birth of 
love in the ruins. Instrumentation: solo oud opens, joined by cello, soft African kora, gentle bass 
strings. No percussion until the final chorus — then soft hand drum heartbeat enters. Style 
reference: Lion King emotional ballad style, slow, spare, and devastating. The city rebuilding in the 
music. Ends with the magic breaking — music fades mid-note.
Page  of 1318

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
SONG 7 — "I SHALL NOT RULE ALONE"
Act 9 | Pages 110–112
Singer(s): Sharak the Shadow King (solo), Nibzu the spider (call-and-response bridge, 
comedic)
SECTION 1 — LYRICS
VERSE 1 (SHARAK):
At last my wait is over, the stars align with fate,
The skies all bow before me, the desert lies in wait.
Half of my life in shadows, my love beneath the sand—
Tonight the world will tremble beneath my guiding hand. 
CHORUS (SHARAK):
For I will be the heartbeat
Of every grain of sand,
The rhythm of the ages,
The ruler of this land.
For half my soul lay buried,
For half my heart was stone—
Tonight the world shall witness
I shall not rule alone. 
BRIDGE — call-and-response (SHARAK + NIBZU):
SHARAK: The stars will bend before me—
NIBZU: Bend, bend, bend!
SHARAK: The clouds will break in fear—
NIBZU: Break, break, break!
SHARAK: And she will walk beside me—
A Queen reborn from sand] 
NIBZU (softly, creepily):
She whispered in your madness…
She always spoke your name… 
SHARAK:
And now the lock is open—
Her heart begins to wake… 
OUTRO (SHARAK — powerful voice):
…Zahra!
SECTION 2 — SCENE CONTEXT & ACTION SEQUENCE
The Shadow King's Throne Room — vast, torchlit with liquid green fire, black and dark-red 
marble floors veined with gold reflecting the flames like oil pools. Sharak has just received the 
fake Talisman from the Djinn, believing he has finally won. His pet spider Nibzu — small, violet-
scaled, enormous googly amber eyes, smiling with stupid devotion — sits beside him.
Sharak snatches the Talisman and lifts it high, laughing violently, dust shaking from the ceiling. 
He presses it to his chest and begins the song, addressing a painted fresco of Zahra on the 
wall. As Verse 1 builds, green fire swirls around him in theatrical spirals. At the Chorus, obsidian 
spider-leg energy formations erupt from the throne floor, framing him like a dark crown. Sharak 
strides across the throne room like a man who has finally arrived at his destiny — dangerous, 
Page  of 1418

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
grand, and utterly unhinged. The Bridge call-and-response with Nibzu is hilariously sinister: 
Nibzu scrambles across the walls to echo each line with tiny gleeful squeaks, nearly falling off a 
pillar. But Nibzu's whispered verses reveal the tragedy beneath the madness — Sharak's love 
for Zahra is real and broken. The outro is raw: Sharak's voice cracks as he calls her name into 
the dark, the echo fading into silence. The Djinn watches from the shadows, heartbroken. 
Upstairs, Aisha hears the name of her mother — and her breath shatters.
SECTION 3 — SUNO AI MUSIC PROMPT
3D animated movie musical, theatrical villain song — grandiose, tragic-comic, unhinged yet 
heartbreaking. Main voice: Sharak — deep cartoonish baritone, commanding and theatrical, like a 
Disney villain at peak self-delusion — slightly broken at the edges, hints of grief beneath the 
megalomaniac grandeur. Second voice: Nibzu spider — tiny, squeaky, comedic call-and-response, 
enthusiastic and slightly dim, contrast with Sharak's gravity. Mood: triumphant madness covering a 
shattered heart — half villain anthem, half desperate love song. Instrumentation: wicked orchestral 
strings opening, heavy brass stabs, pulsing dark percussion, low bass kora drone, green-fire 
ambience, ominous choir. Style reference: Lion King "Be Prepared" dark theatrical energy meets 
tragic obsession. Bridge is comedic before the outro turns raw and vulnerable.
Page  of 1518

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
SONG 8 — "DESERT SUMMONING ANTHEM"
Act 10 | Pages 122–124
Singer(s): Aisha sings wordlessly — ancestral trance-anthem, no intelligible lyrics; Six Sand 
Guardians are summoned
SECTION 1 — LYRICS
[NO LYRICS — This is an instrumental/wordless piece]
Aisha opens her mouth and what emerges is not a lullaby. It is an anthem — ancient, wordless, 
primal. The sands hear her.
SECTION 2 — SCENE CONTEXT & ACTION SEQUENCE
At the base of the great Quazar Al'Zaman Hourglass, the kingdom's balance-keeper, cracked 
open and nearly empty. The Shadow King's forces approach. Aisha falls to her knees, pressing 
her palm to the cold glass and whispering to her mother — "Forgive me. Guide me." Her 
Talisman grows warm, then luminous. Something ancient rises from within her.
She opens her mouth — and the sands respond. A swirling cylindrical wall of golden sand 
erupts around her, sealing the space. Dora dives in just before it closes. The storm outside dies. 
Aisha enters a trance — her spirit transported to the Temple of Light in the spirit realm, where 
she meets her mother Zahra's spirit. Zahra places her forehead to Aisha's — "Your memories 
are coming back. It's time for us to wake up."
Back in the physical world: Aisha stands within the golden swirling sands, still singing — eyes 
open but seeing beyond. From the dunes surrounding the Kingdom Walls, sand erupts upward 
like dancers emerging from water. Six Giant Sand Guardians form — bodies made of kinetic 
sand, golden arabesque patterns flowing like liquid light, faces hidden beneath hoods of swirling 
grains. They circle Aisha, dancing. Their movements trace a giant glowing mandala across the 
sand around her — patterns shifting and glowing as if the desert itself is listening. The anthem 
guides the guardians like a conductor's baton made of pure ancestral memory.
SECTION 3 — SUNO AI MUSIC PROMPT
3D animated movie musical, epic wordless ancestral instrumental anthem, desert theme,  — no 
intelligible lyrics, purely melodic vocalise and instruments. Female voice: young mezzo-soprano 
singing only vowels and ancient tones — not words, but feels like it carries meaning. Haunting, 
ethereal, vast. Mood: ancient awakening, ancestral memory rising, the desert answering a call 
older than history. Ethereal and epic with folk soul. Instrumentation: opens with a single African 
talking drum, joined by kora and oud in a modal folk melody, layered overtone throat singing, then: 
djembe ensemble builds, mbira constellations, strings rising like a tide, finally brass and choir join 
in triumphant formation. Creative fusion: African roots, Middle Eastern melodic modes, orchestral 
swell. Curious, ancient, you don't understand the words but feel the meaning. Awakening music. 
Climaxes in a massive multi-ethnic orchestral surge.
Page  of 1618

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
SONG 9 — "ON THE WINGS OF RISING SAND"
Act 11 | Pages 133–134
Singer(s): Aisha + the Prince (duet), both solo verses, shared chorus and reprise
SECTION 1 — LYRICS
CHORUS (BOTH — opening, before verses):
On the wings of rising sand,
Love awakens shining bright.
Every heartbeat shapes the land,
Every spirit lights a star
In the quiet of the night. 
VERSE (AISHA):
I feel the echoes of the past
Guiding me with every stride.
Voices of the ones before me
Walking softly by my side. 
VERSE (PRINCE):
I followed echoes through the night,
Seeking truth beyond my sight.
If you listen with your heart,
Every shadow turns to light. 
REPRISE — BOTH:
On the wings of rising sand,
Love awakens shining bright.
Every heartbeat shapes the land,
Every spirit lights a star
In the quiet of the night.
SECTION 2 — SCENE CONTEXT & ACTION SEQUENCE
The Grand Ballroom of the Palace of Quazar Al'Zaman — night, filled with lanterns, silk, and 
celebration. The entire court and city are celebrating the restoration of the Hourglass and the 
defeat of the Shadow King. Aisha descends the grand staircase in a pale pink-lavender kaftan 
with golden constellation embroidery, her scarf flowing behind her like dawnlight.
The crowd parts — and there stands the Prince, human, alive, restored. His ivory kaftan with 
golden rims glows softly with warm morning light. His hands tremble slightly. Their reunion is 
wordless for a moment — breath, tears, laughter. The Sultan unites their hands. The orchestra 
begins.
They step into the center of the ballroom and the song opens mid-chorus — the world has 
already changed; love is already there. As Aisha sings her verse, the Talisman glows, tiny 
golden motes of light drifting from it like fireflies woven from memories. As the Prince sings his 
verse, the motes spiral upward into arabesque geometric patterns — calligraphy moving like the 
wind. The reprise builds as the entire court joins in spirit, music swelling to its final luminous 
crescendo. The camera glides past lanterns, dancing couples, Dora lounging like royalty on a 
golden velvet cushion with a crooked gold crown, Niura on a silk pillow with a tiny perfect snake-
sized crown. At the very end: the golden motes float past the balcony, rise to the real night sky 
— and form Arabic calligraphy in the stars that slowly transforms into: AISHA. Fade to black.
SECTION 3 — SUNO AI MUSIC PROMPT
Page  of 1718

Aisha and the Sands of Destiny — Songs Package
3D animated movie musical, romantic finale ballad duet — warm, triumphant, emotionally full. First 
voice: young princess mezzo-soprano, tender and glowing, American style, warm — carries only a 
few notes with deep feeling. Second voice: young Prince tenor, warm and hopeful, gently soaring. 
Together: a tender two-voice ballad, harmonic and light, not over-produced. Mood: earned love, 
hope restored, the world made right, gentle magic lingering. Instrumentation: soft string quartet 
opens, oud melody joins, light kora and mbira, gentle hand drum heartbeat, then full orchestra 
joins for reprise — brass, choral voices humming behind them, rising to a luminous crescendo. 
Style reference: Lion King romantic ballad warmth meets Aladdin's "A Whole New World" floating 
tenderness. Ends on a single sustained note from both voices together, then silence.
Page  of 1818
